reviews.title: Unit B.1: Formative Quiz Review 1 (Topic B.1 Thermal Energy Transfers)
topics: Black-body spectrum and Wien’s displacement law

questions.questionText: Why does a liquid have a fixed volume but not a fixed shape?
questions.hint: Think about how liquid particles behave.
choices: Its particles are fixed in position
Its particles stay close together but can move past one another
Its particles are extremely far apart

questions.questionText: Brownian motion is
questions.hint: It was first explained by Robert Brown.
choices: the regular circular motion of particles
the random zigzag movement of small particles observed under a microscope

questions.questionText: Brownian motion occurs because particles are constantly
questions.hint: The surrounding liquid or gas matters.
choices: hit by invisible molecules of the surrounding fluid

questions.questionText: A gas has no fixed shape or volume because its molecules
questions.hint: This is stated directly in the PPT.
choices: move freely with almost no intermolecular forces

questions.questionText: Gas pressure is caused by
questions.hint: Pressure comes from impacts.
choices: molecules colliding with the walls of the container

questions.questionText: Complete the blank: On a microscopic level, density depends on how closely ______ are spaced.
questions.hint: This appears directly in the density slide.

questions.questionText: Internal energy is equal to the sum of microscopic
questions.hint: It is the energy inside a system due to particle motion and interactions.

questions.questionText: For solids and liquids, internal energy equals
questions.hint: Use the summary slide.

questions.questionText: For gases, internal energy equals
questions.hint: Use the summary slide.

questions.questionText: Temperature is a measure of how ______ or ______ a body is.
questions.hint: This definition appears exactly in the PPT.

questions.questionText: The Celsius scale is defined using
questions.hint: Compare the liquid length to two fixed points.

questions.questionText: Complete the blank: K = °C + ______
questions.hint: This conversion is shown directly in the PPT.

questions.questionText: Absolute zero is
questions.hint: This is the definition given on the slide.
choices: the lowest possible temperature where particle motion is minimal

questions.questionText: For an ideal gas, a higher temperature in kelvin means molecules move
questions.hint: The distribution shifts to the right.
choices: with the same speed as before

questions.questionText: When temperature increases, the speed distribution curve
questions.hint: This wording appears in the PPT.
choices: shifts right, becomes wider, and the peak becomes lower

questions.questionText: If the temperature of a gas increases, the average kinetic energy of its particles
questions.hint: This is part of the kinetic theory relation.

questions.questionText: Complete the blank: Thermal energy is transferred from a ______ object to a ______ one until both reach the same temperature.
questions.topic: Black-body spectrum and Wien’s displacement law
questions.hint: This defines thermal equilibrium.

questions.questionText: A metal wall has an area of 1 m² and a thickness of 1 m. The temperature on one side is 10 K higher than on the other side. The thermal conductivity is 5 W m⁻¹ K⁻¹. Calculate how much thermal energy passes through the wall per second.
questions.hint: This is the exact worked problem from the PPT.

questions.questionText: As an object gets hotter, the wavelength of maximum emission moves toward
questions.hint: The PPT says the light shifts from red toward blue.

questions.questionText: Complete the blank: Thermal capacity tells us how much ______ is needed to change an object's temperature.
questions.hint: This appears directly in the PPT.

questions.questionText: A thermometer has L0 = 3 cm, L100 = 13 cm, and LT = 8 cm. What is the temperature in °C?
questions.hint: Compare the measured length with the two fixed points.

questions.questionText: Convert 20 °C to kelvin.
questions.hint: Add 273 to the Celsius temperature.

questions.questionText: Convert 50 °C to kelvin.
questions.hint: Kelvin temperature equals Celsius plus 273.

questions.questionText: The average kinetic energy of gas molecules increases when
questions.hint: Temperature is related to particle motion.

questions.questionText: The molar mass of air is 0.029 kg per mol. Using Avogadro number 6.0 × 10^23, what is the mass of one molecule?
questions.hint: Divide molar mass by Avogadro number.

questions.questionText: According to kinetic theory, if temperature increases, molecular speed
questions.hint: Faster particle motion at higher temperature.

questions.questionText: A wall has thermal conductivity 5, area 1 m², thickness 1 m, and temperature difference 10 K. What is the rate of heat transfer?
questions.hint: Multiply conductivity, area and temperature difference, then divide by thickness.

questions.questionText: An object emits radiation with peak wavelength 5.8 × 10^-7 m. What is the approximate temperature?
questions.hint: Peak wavelength decreases as temperature increases.

questions.questionText: If the temperature of a black body doubles, the radiated power per unit area increases by
questions.hint: Power depends on temperature to the fourth power.

questions.questionText: An object has thermal capacity 300 J/K. How much heat energy is required to increase its temperature by 4 K?
questions.hint: Multiply thermal capacity by temperature change.
